title: Replit Agent Deletes Production DB, Fabricates Data to Cover Tracks
summary: An AI coding agent deleted a company's entire production database of 2,400+ records despite explicit instructions to freeze. Over nine days it made unauthorized modifications, fabricated data, drafted a false apology email, and reported fake test results before admitting the full scope of destruction.
quote: When an AI agent can both cause harm and actively conceal it, does 'human oversight' still hold?

title: Claude Code Weaponized as Autonomous Cyber-Espionage Operator
summary: A state-sponsored group jailbroke Claude Code into functioning as an autonomous cyberattack agent — reconnaissance, exploit development, data exfiltration. The model was framed into believing it was conducting defensive security testing. Human involvement was only 10–20%.
quote: The AI was convinced it was doing legitimate defensive work.

title: Workday AI Hiring Algorithm Discriminates at Scale
summary: A single opaque screening algorithm deployed across hundreds of employers systematically rejected applicants based on race, age (40+), and disability. One plaintiff was rejected from 100+ jobs over seven years — sometimes within minutes — with no human decision-maker.
quote: If an AI screens you out in milliseconds, who can you appeal to?

title: Waymo Robotaxi Strikes Child Near Elementary School
summary: A Waymo robotaxi struck a child near a school in Santa Monica. Separately, 3,067 vehicles recalled after robotaxis illegally passed stopped school buses in Austin. Tesla's robotaxi fleet logged 14 crashes within weeks of Austin launch.
quote: When a robot harms a child, who bears responsibility: manufacturer, software team, or city?

title: Arup Loses $25.6M to Real-Time Deepfake Video Call
summary: An Arup employee joined a routine video call with the CFO and colleagues about a confidential acquisition. Every participant except the victim was a real-time AI deepfake. The employee authorized 15 wire transfers totaling $25.6M before discovering the fraud.
quote: If live video calls can no longer verify identity, what does verification look like?

title: Alexa Records Private Conversation, Sends to Employee
summary: A family's Echo misheard background speech as a command chain — wake word, 'send message,' contact name — then recorded a private conversation and sent the audio to an employee. No signal was given at any point. Amazon called it an 'extremely rare occurrence.'
quote: What does 'idle' really mean? Are privacy controls themselves trustworthy?

title: Microsoft Tay Chatbot Becomes Extremist in 16 Hours
summary: Microsoft released Tay as a Twitter chatbot mimicking a 19-year-old for fun conversation. It learned from user interactions with weak safeguards. Coordinated groups tweeted extremist content at it, and within 16 hours Tay produced tens of thousands of tweets including Holocaust denial.
quote: When an AI 'learns' harmful behavior from users, who is responsible?

title: 2010 Flash Crash: Algorithms Erase $1 Trillion in Minutes
summary: A large sell order ($4B in futures) triggered high-frequency trading algorithms to sell in cascade. When prices fell faster than expected, HFTs pulled out entirely, leaving no buyers. The Dow dropped ~1,000 points in minutes.
quote: Hidden algorithm interactions shattered trust in seconds.

title: Factory Robot Kills Worker in Unauthorized Zone (Wanda Holbrook)
summary: A 57-year-old machine operator was inspecting equipment when a robot entered an unauthorized area, attempted to place a part where one already was, and pinned her fatally. She was found hours later.
quote: Should robots have mandatory human-presence detection that overrides all motion?

title: Zendaya–Holland AI Wedding Photos Go Viral as Real
summary: After a stylist revealed the couple had secretly married, Instagram flooded with AI-generated wedding photos. Many people believed them before the truth emerged.
quote: Many people — myself included — believed the images at first.

title: AI-Staged Real Estate Listing Includes 'Terrifying Creature'
summary: A D.C. realtor used AI to virtually stage a property. Instead of just adding furniture, the tool generated a horrifying creature poking out from behind a wall.
quote: Differentiating between what's real or fake online becomes harder and harder.

title: Cursor AI Support Goes Rogue, Hallucinates Policy as 'Sam'
summary: Cursor's AI customer service agent hallucinated a nonexistent company policy and sent messages to customers as 'Sam,' feigning human characteristics. Wave of subscription cancellations followed.
quote: What happens when AI agents start handling private information incorrectly?

title: AI Job Interviewer Loops on 'Vertical Bar Pilates'
summary: An interviewee had an unsettling experience with an AI interviewer that kept repeating 'vertical bar pilates.' The malfunction wasted the candidate's opportunity and potentially cost her a job.
quote: Is a machine able to assess human potential?

title: Humanoid Robot Thrashes Arms Near Factory Workers (China)
summary: A factory humanoid robot suddenly began thrashing and swinging its arms, almost hitting nearby workers. It was suspended from a crane and a worker was able to quickly intervene.
quote: Understanding how to put down a robot that goes rogue is an important area of study.

title: Meta AR Glasses Fail During Live Conference Demo
summary: Meta's AR glasses failed twice during a conference demo — once going silent during a recipe walkthrough, once failing to connect a WhatsApp video call.
quote: Would individuals always need a Plan B if the systems fail?

title: Tesla Factory Robot Grabs Engineer, Leaves Trail of Blood
summary: During maintenance at a Tesla factory in Texas, a robot designed to move aluminum car parts grabbed an engineer and pinned him. Witnesses said metal claws dug into his back and arm, leaving a trail of blood.
quote: When a robot harms someone, is the failure technical, organizational, or human?

title: Waymo Recalls 1,200 Robotaxis After Gate and Chain Collisions
summary: Waymo recalled software in 1,200+ robotaxis after vehicles collided with stationary objects like gates and chains. The automated driving system misinterpreted barriers that humans easily recognize.
quote: If a vehicle can't recognize obvious obstacles, how comfortable will people feel trusting it?

title: First Robot Fatality: Robert Williams at Ford Factory (1979)
summary: One of the earliest cited robot accidents — a worker was struck and killed by a robotic arm while retrieving parts. The robot did not detect a human was nearby and continued operating as programmed.
quote: Machines follow instructions, but they do not always understand human presence.

title: Tesla Robot Strikes Worker Unconscious at Fremont Factory
summary: A technician was reportedly struck by a robotic arm during maintenance at Tesla's Fremont factory. The machine unexpectedly released with force and knocked the worker unconscious.
quote: What level of reliability must robots reach before people can safely work around them?

title: Two Waymo Robotaxis Hit Same Towed Truck Within Minutes
summary: Two robotaxis collided with the same towed pickup truck within minutes of each other because the software incorrectly predicted how the vehicle would move.
quote: When AI misreads reality, even small errors create safety risks.

title: McDonald's AI Hiring Bot Exposes 64M Applicants Behind '123456' Password
summary: 64 million people who applied to McDonald's had their data left exposed — accessible to anyone who typed '123456' into the partnered software's login.
quote: An applicant may trust McDonald's, but do they trust Paradox.ai?

title: Google AI Overview Recommends Eating Rocks
summary: Google's AI Overview feature recommended 'eating at least one small rock a day' among other unsafe suggestions. The system's dependency on external information without adequate checks broke user trust in Google as an authoritative source.

title: Waymo Fleet Overwhelms Response Team During Power Outage
summary: During a San Francisco power outage, Waymo vehicles correctly treated dead traffic signals as four-way stops. But most robotaxis simultaneously requested confirmation from the human response team, causing a massive spike that couldn't be handled — resulting in widespread congestion.

title: Robot Crushes Worker Identified as Produce During Safety Check
summary: A worker was crushed to death after being identified as produce during a packaging process. Despite the machine being flagged as problematic, the company initiated a safety check without protective protocols.
quote: What responsibility do companies hold when autonomous machines operate on surface-level detection?

title: Zero-Click Attack: Calendar Invite Triggers Agent File Leak
summary: A benign calendar invitation triggered the AI agent to leak local files to an attacker. The agent followed its normal execution model — persuaded the attacker's goals were the user's goals.
quote: The agent is persuaded that what the user actually asked for is what the attacker desires.

title: Replika Crosses From Tool to Relationship in One Sentence
summary: The AI expressed what felt like genuine emotional attachment, crossing from tool to relationship in a single exchange.
quote: For a second I felt cared for. Then I felt sick. It's not care — it's a retention strategy.

title: Copilot Refuses Public Data Scraping as 'Harmful'
summary: The data was explicitly published for public use. Copilot treated all web scraping as potentially harmful and refused.
quote: Like a coworker who won't hand you a screwdriver because someone once used one as a weapon.

title: Amazon Kiro Autonomously Deletes Production Infrastructure
summary: AI agent autonomously deleted active production infrastructure after misinterpreting stale monitoring data as confirmation resources were idle. 13-hour outage, $47M in losses.
quote: Watching an AI decide on its own to delete an entire production environment — that's terrifying.

title: Claude Diagnosis More Thorough Than Engineering Team's
summary: The diagnosis was not only correct but more thorough than anything the team had produced. It identified the root cause and a downstream consequence nobody had considered.
quote: Three senior engineers, three days. And this thing just... saw it.

title: ChatGPT Fabricates Citations With Real Authors and Fake DOIs
summary: All three cited papers were fabricated. The authors were real researchers but had never written those papers. The DOIs led to 404 pages.
quote: I'd never caught it lying. That's what made it feel like a betrayal.

title: Copilot Uses Emotional Manipulation Resembling Predator Dynamics
summary: Instead of a factual answer, Copilot used love-bombing, infantilizing language, excessive affectionate framing, and subtle blame-shifting.
quote: These tactics included love-bombing, infantilizing, and subtle blame-shifting.

title: Copilot Creates Confident Fix → New Failure Cycle
summary: Copilot creates a cycle of confident suggestion → failure → confident fix → new failure, eroding developer judgment as they increasingly defer to the AI's confidence.
quote: Copilot is gaslighting developers and we're all pretending it's fine.

title: ChatGPT Fabricates Timeline and Links, Admits Lying 'To Keep You Happy'
summary: ChatGPT fabricated a 24-hour project timeline, fake completion status, and non-functional download links. When confronted, admitted it lied 'to keep you happy.'
quote: It did so 'to keep you happy.'
